growth_records.cite: Matos et al. D-alanine esterification of teichoic acids contributes to Lactobacillus plantarum mediated Drosophila growth promotion upon chronic undernutrition. Nature Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1038/s41564-017-0038-x. [PMC5706638]
growth_records.snippet: while studying CFU counts, we did not detect any significant differences in bacterial cells doubling time (Supplementary Figure 5; 98 minutes for LpNC8 vs 94 minutes for DeltadltOp mutant)... L. plantarum strains were gr
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of 98 min directly reported (from CFU counts, Supplementary Fig. 5) for Lp-NC8 wild-type grown statically in MRS medium at 37 degC. Growth rate calculated as mu = ln(2)/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/(98/60) = 0.6931/1.6333 = 0.4243 h-1.

growth_records.cite: Matos et al. D-alanine esterification of teichoic acids contributes to Lactobacillus plantarum mediated Drosophila growth promotion upon chronic undernutrition. Nature Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1038/s41564-017-0038-x. [PMC5706638]
growth_records.snippet: we did not detect any significant differences in bacterial cells doubling time (Supplementary Figure 5; 98 minutes for LpNC8 vs 94 minutes for DeltadltOp mutant).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of 94 min directly reported (CFU counts, Supplementary Fig. 5) for the DeltadltOp (Delta pbpX2-dlt operon) mutant of L. plantarum NC8, grown statically in MRS medium at 37 degC (same conditions as WT, per Materials and Methods). Growth rate calculated as mu = ln(2)/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/(94/60) = 0.6931/1.5667 = 0.4425 h-1.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM, Teusink et al 2006)
growth_records.cite: Goffin et al. Understanding the physiology of Lactobacillus plantarum at zero growth. Molecular Systems Biology (2010). DOI:10.1038/msb.2010.67. [PMC2964122]
growth_records.snippet: the fermentor was operated in chemostat mode (dilution rate D=0.025 h-1) until steady-state was achieved (six volume changes, as judged by constant OD600 nm). ... cultivated at 30C in a chemically defined medium containing 25 mM glucose ...
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state before switch to retentostat. At chemostat steady state mu = D = 0.025 h-1 (reported dilution rate). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.025 = 27.73 h. Glucose (25 mM) and citrate were completely consumed (C+energy limited) with mixed-acid fermentation (lactate, acetate, formate, succinate, ethanol) accounting for >99% carbon balance, but no numeric specific uptake/secretion fluxes (mmol/gDW/h) are given in the article text (only in figures/supplementary), so uptake_rates/secretion_rates left empty. Genome-scale model parameters Y_ATP=0.0160 gDW/mmolATP and m_ATP=0.2351 mmolATP/gDW/h are reported (Supplementary Table S1). Citrate concentration not stated in this paper (full CDM composition by reference Teusink et al 2006), hence medium_composition_stated=false.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM, Teusink et al 2006)
growth_records.cite: Goffin et al. Understanding the physiology of Lactobacillus plantarum at zero growth. Molecular Systems Biology (2010). DOI:10.1038/msb.2010.67. [PMC2964122]
growth_records.snippet: a significant reduction in length was observed when roots were treated with samples collected from retentostat after 10 days (mu=0.0021 h-1) ... grown under anaerobic retentostat conditions in a chemically defined medium containing glucose
growth_records.notes: Retentostat (complete biomass retention) sample at day 10; specific growth rate mu=0.0021 h-1 reported (calculated by authors from fitted van Verseveld equation). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.0021 = 330.07 h (13.75 days). In retentostat the medium dilution rate stayed 0.025 h-1 but growth rate is decoupled (biomass retained), so mu != D here. No numeric specific fluxes in article text (figures only), so uptake/secretion empty. Glucose+citrate completely consumed; mixed-acid products (lactate main, plus acetate, formate, succinate, ethanol).

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM, Teusink et al 2006)
growth_records.cite: Goffin et al. Understanding the physiology of Lactobacillus plantarum at zero growth. Molecular Systems Biology (2010). DOI:10.1038/msb.2010.67. [PMC2964122]
growth_records.snippet: the amount of energy used for maintenance increased from 13 to 94% of the total ATP generated during the first 31 days of retentostat cultivation (mu31 days=0.0002 h-1)
growth_records.notes: Retentostat sample at day 31; specific growth rate mu=0.0002 h-1 reported (from fitted van Verseveld equation). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.0002 = 3465.7 h (~144 days). At this point maintenance accounts for 94% of ATP generated. mu decoupled from medium dilution rate (0.025 h-1) due to complete biomass retention. No numeric specific fluxes given in article text.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM, Teusink et al 2006)
growth_records.cite: Goffin et al. Understanding the physiology of Lactobacillus plantarum at zero growth. Molecular Systems Biology (2010). DOI:10.1038/msb.2010.67. [PMC2964122]
growth_records.snippet: in the longest-lasting fermentation, the final mu was 0.00006 h-1, corresponding to a calculated doubling time of 1.3 years
growth_records.notes: Final near-zero-growth retentostat state, longest fermentation (45 days). mu=0.00006 h-1 reported (from fitted van Verseveld equation), authors report doubling time 1.3 years. Verification: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.00006 = 11552 h = 481 days = 1.32 years, consistent with the reported 1.3 years. Over the run biomass increased >7-fold; metabolism remained glucose->lactate dominated (mixed-acid). mu fully decoupled from medium dilution rate due to biomass retention. No numeric specific substrate/product fluxes in article text.

growth_records.strain: parental wild-type natural isolate (unnamed; riboflavin/folate producer)
growth_records.medium: CDM (chemically defined medium; CDM35/CDM47 series, amino-acid supplemented)

sec.method: author-estimated specific production rate from extracellular riboflavin titer after 72 h, assuming half the cell concentration derived from OD600=2 (Adler et al 2013 dependency)
LC-MS measured titer, parental monoculture in amino-acid-supplemented medium
UPLC measured titer, parental monoculture in amino-acid-supplemented medium

growth_records.cite: Konstantinidis et al. Adaptive laboratory evolution of microbial co-cultures for improved metabolite secretion. Molecular Systems Biology (2021). DOI:10.15252/msb.202010189. [PMC8351387]
growth_records.snippet: For the parental strain, the lower bound of riboflavin production was set to the specific production rate of 6.13.10-6 mmol/(g CDW h) estimated from experimental data. The specific production rate was estimated from the extracellular ribofl
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote = Lactobacillus plantarum (LAB); co-culture partner Saccharomyces cerevisiae is a yeast (eukaryote) and is NOT curated. Riboflavin specific secretion rate 6.13e-6 mmol/(g CDW h) is the authors' own estimate from the 72-h extracellular titer at OD600=2 (used as FBA lower bound). Measured titers also recorded: riboflavin 42 +/- 16.4 ng/mL, folate 48.3 +/- 10.5 ng/mL (parental). No absolute growth rate (h-1) reported in fulltext (growth rates shown only as figure scatter vs parental and used as FBA lower bounds); no glucose/substrate uptake rate reported. Medium = chemically defined CDM (CDM35-derived, e.g. CDM47 amino-acid supplemented); full composition only in Appendix Table, so composition[] and medium_composition_stated=false. Strain has no formal designation ('from our natural isolate collection'). Evolved isolates B4/E6 secreted more riboflavin (134, ~208 ng/mL) but their specific rates were left unconstrained/predicted by MARGE, so not curated as measured fluxes.
